front: Play: Louis Ironson abandons his AIDS-stricken lover Prior Walter for the closeted Mormon Joe Pitt; subtitled 'A Gay Fantasia on National Themes'
Play: the ghost of Ethel Rosenberg sings a lullaby for Roy Cohn as he dies of AIDS
Play: Roy Cohn declares 'I wish I was an octopus,' claims labels reflect one's 'clout,' and offers a job to a closeted Mormon
Play: Harper hallucinates visits to Antarctica with her imaginary travel agent Mr. Lies while addicted to Valium
Play: an angel delivers the message to 'stop moving' after the 1906 San Francisco earthquake, when God abandoned heaven
Play: stage directions require same actor to portray Belize and the imaginary Mr. Lies; another actor portrays Hannah and Rabbi Chemelwitz
Play: set in Agnes Eggling's apartment; action cuts between the fall of the Weimar Republic and 1980s New York City
back: A Bright Room Called Day (Tony Kushner)

front: Play: title blends books by G.B. Shaw and Mary Baker Eddy (shortened to iHo); suicidal longshoreman Gus has children named Pill, Empty, and V
Play: a woman called Homebody dies in Kabul; another fantasizes about sleeping with a hat salesman missing three fingers
